The book of Kings describes more fully the destruction of idolatry that Josiah carried out. His observance of Passover is described here. The Lord’s Supper resembles Passover more than any other Jewish festival; and proper observance of that ordinance shows increasing piety and devotion. God alone can truly make our hearts holy and prepare them for his services; but there are duties we have to do, by which we obtain this blessing from the Lord.

The Scripture does not condemn Josiah’s actions in opposing Pharaoh. Yet Josiah seems deserving of blame for not inquiring of the Lord after he was warned; his death may be a rebuke for his rashness, but it was a judgment on a hypocritical and wicked people. Whoever lives a life of repentance, faith, and obedience cannot be affected by the sudden way in which they are removed. The people mourned for him. Many grieve over sufferings who will not forsake the sins that caused God to send them; yet this alone can turn away judgments. If we blame Josiah’s conduct, we should be watchful so that we are not cut down in a way dishonorable to our profession.
